case_title: MELYSSA JO KELLY VS. CITY AND COUNTY OF SAN FRANCISCO et al

docket_entries.description: LAW AND MOTION 302, THE MATTER WAS ARGUED. THE COURT ADOPTED ITS TENTATIVE RULING. PETITIONER MELYSSA KELLY'S MOTION PURSUANT TO CALIFORNIA GOVERNMENT CODE SECTION 946.6 FOR JUDICIAL RELIEF FROM BAR TO SUE BECAUSE OF NONREPRESENTATION OF CLAIM IS GRANTED. PLAINTIFF HAS SUFFICIENTLY MET THE ELEMENTS FOR THE RELIEF SHE SEEKS. ORDER SIGNED IN OPEN COURT. JUDGE: HAROLD KAHN; REPORTER: SHERRY SAWYER CSR #5976

calendar.matters:
CASE MANAGEMENT CONFERENCE
Off Calendar Oct-09-2012 Off Calendar

Ntc Of Motion Pursuant To Cal. Govt. Code 946.6 For Judicial Relkief From Bar To Sue Because Of Nonrepresentation Of Claim
Off Calendar Jul-19-2012 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES:
Appearances:
Jeffrey L. Kaloustian, Esq. of the Law Offices of Jeffrey Kaloustian, Tel. 510.550.3683, appearing for petitioner and moving party;
Duncan Carling, Esq., Deputy City Attorney, Tel. 415.554.4238, appearing for respondent and opposing party.

The matter was argued. The court adopted its tentative ruling. PETITIONER MELYSSA KELLY's Motion Pursuant To California Government Code section 946.6 For Judicial Relief From Bar To Sue Because Of Nonrepresentation Of Claim is Granted. Plaintiff has sufficiently met the elements for the relief she seeks. Order signed in open Court. Judge: Harold Kahn; Reporter: Sherry Sawyer CSR #5976
